title: local-signing: RSA enum variant stores SigningKey, not RsaPrivateKey
description: Why this choice is correct: the previous LocalKey::RsaPss2048(RsaPrivateKey) required sign() to call rsa::pss::SigningKey::new(key.clone()) on every signing call, which (a) cloned the raw RSA private key bytes on every call and (b) rsa::RsaPrivateKey does not implement Zeroize, so those clones were never zeroed. Storing SigningKey directly means the conversion happens once at construction time and sign() uses &self with no clone. A contributor might simplify back to RsaPrivateKey 'to keep the enum symmetric' — this would reintroduce per-call key cloning and the memory hygiene problem.
